Transcrição Completa: Control your Life with Brandon Carter

[06:41] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Leading vs Lagging Indicators - The Truth"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Conceito fundamental - Leading Indicators (o que vai acontecer) vs Lagging Indicators (o que aconteceu), comparação com KPIs de negócios
[06:43] Here's the truth. I'm going break it down. It's super simple. You ain't got to do all this hocus pocus. It's super success is super simple in all areas. All you have to do is look at the leading indicators and the lagging indicators of life. The leading indicators.
[06:58] So, basically what I'm saying is a leading indicator. Okay. No, a lagging indicator is like a goal. That's a goal. When you see somebody who's won a championship or if you see a motherfucker with a bunch of money in the bank, all it does is it shows it shows what happened, right?
[07:16] Like your compliments. They're lagging indicators. You see a motherfucker with a bunch of muscle, you can safely assume he or she has worked out for a significant amount of time and watch their diet. Am I right?
[07:29] Did it matter if they did? Did they have to do a Iaska retreat? Do No, they didn't do none of that shit right? You know, it it doesn't matter if they make their bed or not, right?
[07:40] The lagging indicators are the things that lead to the accomplishments. That's what's important. We got to track these. Like when my man was showing the the the Facebook shit right? You know, showing all the KPIs. You guys understand this with business, right? You look at the KPIs to see if the ads performing well, right?
[07:58] And if they and if the those numbers are right, then you know you're probably running a successful campaign. Am I right? It's probably going to be a good month. It's the same shit in all areas in life and and I'm going to explain that too.
[08:12] So the leading indicators will show you what's going to happen in the future. So it's like predicting the future, right? And lagging indicators to show you what happened. See your mother go all fat. All right. You can assume he's been eating candies and cakes like a child for a significant amount of time, right? He hasn't been watching his diet.

VISUAL: Foto de homem em excelente forma física, lista de leading indicators específicos: track macros/calories daily, track caloric expenditure, protein intake, sleep, calorie deficit

[08:48] All we got to do is look what would be the leading indicators. How would we predict that? Right? Well, if he tracks his macros and his calories every day, right? If he tracks his caloric expenditure every day, if he gets a gram of protein per pound of body weight, seven, eight hours of sleep per night, burns more calories than he consume. Oh, burns more calories than he consumes.

[21:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Why Tracking Is Critical - Peter Drucker Quote"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Quote de Peter Drucker: "If you can't measure it, you can't manage it" + Gym Jesus King Keo: "If you're not tracking, you slacking"
[21:30] And here's why measuring this shit is important. For real. Peter Ducker said, y'all know Peter Ducker, OP, where he was saying, "If you can't measure it, you can't manage it." You know what I'm saying?
[21:42] like these the when you find out what these leading indicators are and you measure them, then you can manage them and then you can actually not only predict the future, but now you can really grab a hold of it and start manipulating the future. You get what I'm saying? And it doesn't all that other shit doesn't matter. You feel me?
[22:00] And yeah, if you can't measure it, you can't manage it. Another guy, Gymnasium Jesus, King Keo, he said, "If you not if you're not tracking, you slacking." hope and uh there's a lot of truth in that statement. There's a lot of truth in that statement.
[22:16] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Hawthorne Effect - Performance Under Observation"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado (Industrial Revolution era)
VISUAL: Conceito de Henry Ford factories, pesquisa de Hawthorne, produtividade dispara quando observada, volta ao normal quando observação para
[22:18] But there's another reason why tracking is so important. There's something called the Hawthorne effect. So it was back in the industrial revolution. Henry Ford and them boys, they started making factories, you know, assembly lines.
[22:31] And a lot of the and Hawthorne, he was a researcher named Hawthorne and his him and his homies, they was trying to figure out why some of these factories outperformed others, even though they were doing basically the same thing.
[22:44] And so when they would go to each factory, Hawthorne and the homies would be like, "Listen guys, don't change nothing. We just want to observe you. Just do your thing normal. We just we're just we're just watching." Every time they went to a new factory, productivity skyrocketed. It just went up to like record levels. And as soon as they left, it went back to normal.
[23:05] Why does that happen? Because people perform better when they're being observed. Does that make sense? It's kind of like, you know, you guys may have the experience when I was like, you know, a kid, you know, fourth, fifth grade, the teacher would lead a class and then what happens, right?
[23:20] Kids start acting crazy. Motherfucker start rolling dice, motherfucker is twerking and shit And then then the teacher come back, motherfucker goes right back to normal. And people perform better when they're being observed.
[23:32] When you start tracking everything like the way I'm I the way I propose you start doing you're the observer right if you're tracking your calories is you it's difficult to eat that shit if you know you got to write it down am I right you get what I'm saying
[23:47] if you see you can see early fucking I'm not spending enough time with my girl and then you can make the adjustments before it gets out of hand you feel me right the same way you do with your ad with an ad account oh man the CPM is too high on this motherfucker let's make make an adjustment.
[24:04] You can start doing that shit with your life. It's like, "Oh, fucking My macros is all fucked up. You can change it before it gets out of hand. Before it's a visible problem. Is this making sense?" Right? Before it start before the problem starts manifesting or if you're on the right track, it's like, "Fuck yeah, I'm doing this shit."
[24:23] And it gives you a it also when you when you're hitting the targets, when you're hitting these lead indicators, it gives you a different kind of confidence, right? Because one, it's like you're actually doing what you say you're going to do. here.
[24:37] You know, when people when people like don't believe in themselves or don't have a lot of confidence, it's like it's usually for a good reason. You know what I'm saying? It's usually just they haven't given them a reason to be proud of themselves. They haven't given themselves a reason to be proud of himself.
[24:52] But you but if you can see the evidence, damn, I'm doing this shit every day. Like, you'll start feeling good about yourself even before the results come. Does that make sense? It's like, "Fuck, I'm following through." Right? And you'll perform better when you see it. Right? But check Oh, one more thing about one more thing before I get the next thing.
[25:12] Also, if you're looking at the lagging indicators, right, the goal, some of y'all got some big goals, right? And it can be you can you can you can look at it the distance you are from it and be discouraged. Does that ever happen to any of y'all? I know I know it's happened to me. It's like, fucking man. Am I ever going to get there?
[25:32] You know, or then you see somebody like flexing on you and shit So, who's doing the things you want to do and it's like sometimes you can feel down about yourself. Because if if your self-esteem or emotions is attached to this future goal, then it's like, "Oh man, I won't be happy until I get there."
[25:48] But when you start tracking the leading indicators and you now I just I just have to make today a victory. I just got to win today. Like I don't even have to look at the future goal. I just have to make today a victory. Does that make sense?
[26:03] When I before when I had like, you know, less than 100,000 YouTube subscribers, like a million seems so far away, but it's like now I just worry about today. And I I feel like that's a lot more motivating. Does that make sense?
[26:17] if you're like super overweight or something or you're not in it's like fucking man I gota lose all this weight. I'm like no no no we just focusing on today. We just we just got to make today a victory. And I've seen that work with like the people I've trained over the years.
[26:32] You know it's like oh man um losing weight so slow. No no man. You losing weight exactly at the rate you should motherfucker one to two pounds a week. But like let's not think about that today. Let's think about today. Let's think about what we're going to do today and make today a victory.
[26:48] And then I I would see him. I would check their shit at the end of the day. Hey, man. Great job tonight. Let's do it again tomorrow. You can see you see how that's more motivating if you just look at what's right in front of you. Does it make sense? All right.
[27:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Skill Acquisition Matrix - The 10,000 Hour Rule"
NÚMEROS: 100 hours = not bad, 1,000 hours = good, 2,000 hours = really good, 5,000 hours = amazing, 10,000 hours = expert (world class), 20,000 hours = legendary (best ever)
VISUAL: Livros "Outliers" (Malcolm Gladwell), "The Talent Code", "Talent is Overrated", matriz de horas necessárias para cada nível de habilidade
[27:05] Now, here's the other thing, though. Here's How many who has any skills they're trying to get like really good at right now? Anyone? Everyone else is perfect. All right, cool. Um, with those of y'all who want who want to improve, um, I'm g show you how to master any skill.
[27:24] Who who's heard of this book, Outliers, by the homie Malcolm Gladwell. There's a lot of books on this topic. There's this one. There's another great one called The Talent Code, and there's an even better one called Talent is Overrated, but they all kind of talk about some of the same thing.
[27:39] It's you need about 10,000 hours to be an expert. You've heard this, like you get 10,000 hours, you you'll be uh one of the best in the world. Well, guess what, man? That's just math, isn't it?
[27:52] Like, check it out. So, I came up with this thing called the the skill acquisition matrix. Now, most things you don't need to be a motherfucker one of the best in the world at like 10,000. You don't need 10,000 hours on most things to be really good, right?
[28:07] I think that a 100 hours and I I have this based off what I read in like life experience. I think a 100 hours makes you not bad, right? Who who plays any of y'all play any instruments, right? You know, somebody plays what what do you play? Yeah.
[28:24] Somebody played piano every day for like three months, they'd be like they might not be great, but they you there'll be noticeable progress, right? Yeah. I think you know 3 months about 90 days about these numbers around it but I think it makes you like not bad right
[28:42] I think a th000 hours makes you good like oh that that motherfucker's good you know what I'm saying 2,000 hours oh that person's really good 5,000 hours is amazing right motherfucker you getting panties thrown at you all types of shit 10,000 hours obviously one of the best
[29:00] And I think 20,000 hours, this is when you become like legendary, one of the best ever. Most of us probably won't get 20,000 hours in most things. We probably won't get 20,000 hours in anything important, right? Some of y'all may have 20,000 Call of Duty hours or some dumb shit right now, right?
[29:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Skill Acquisition Matrix - Time Breakdown"
NÚMEROS: 1 hora/dia = 3 meses para "not bad", 2 horas/dia = 1.7 meses, 4 horas/dia = 25 dias (números arredondados)
VISUAL: Tabela matemática mostrando diferentes níveis de habilidade vs horas diárias de prática necessárias, breakdown completo de 100h até 20,000h
[29:20] So, what does that look like? What does the math look like? Then it just becomes a math problem at that point, right? Hour a day, three months makes you not bad. Two hours a day. You get there 1.7 months. And these numbers are rounded. Don't be factchecking my math nerds. Like I'm I'm rounding up. All right. Four hours a day, you can get there in about 25 days. Right?
[29:45] It's just a math problem at this point, right? I'm not going to go through all of this. You can take a picture or screenshot or something. But now, now we can make it real. We can we it's 10,000 hours is just arbitrary. Oh yeah, it's a good idea. Malcolm Gladwell, right? But what what if you motherfucker set the goal based off hours and then track it? Does it make sense?
[30:08] So sometimes you can be you can some people will look at this and be discouraged like fucking I don't want to wait that long to get good. But I see it I re kind of reframe that. You know there'll be times where I'm trying to like acquire a new skill and I'll start to feel like fucking I'm not really good yet.
[30:25] But then I because I track how many hours I worked on it, I'm like, "Oh, I'm not even supposed to be good yet." Like, "This is where I'm this is where I'm supposed to be given the amount of time I put in." Does that make sense? And then it just becomes a math problem because every everything is math. 📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Brandon: 42 anos, fazendo milhões há anos
Sales Team Performance: 59% close rate (pessoas que falam), 28% close rate total, 15% no-show rate
Mês exemplo: $800K contracted revenue, $600K cash collected upfront
Social Media: 1M+ YouTube followers, 1M+ Instagram followers (ambos no mesmo ano)
YouTube posting: 80% consistency (quase diário)
Personal close rate: 80% (anos fazendo vendas pessoalmente antes de equipe)
Sales experience: Desde 16 anos
Fitness: 3,500 calories = 1 lb fat, 1 gram protein/lb body weight, 7-8 hours sleep
Relationship tracking: 2.7 horas/dia quality time (baixo, usual 3.5h), 90% do mês sem conflito
Family: Filho em Atlanta, voa a cada 2 semanas + todos eventos
Skill Acquisition Matrix:
100 horas = not bad (~3 meses @ 1h/dia)
1,000 horas = good
2,000 horas = really good
5,000 horas = amazing
10,000 horas = expert/world class
20,000 horas = legendary/best ever
Time Allocation: 168 horas/semana total, exemplo participante 50h livres
Sales team meetings: 8 AM diário
Guitar practice: 1 hora/dia (5-6 AM)
Average American: 17 lbs overweight, <$1,000 banco
Team interruptions: Apenas 3 pessoas podem interromper (namorada, mãe do filho, CMO)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
CORE PHILOSOPHY:
Life is Math - Tudo pode ser quantificado e previsto
Four Quadrants of Life:
Money
Health & Fitness
Sexual Relationships
Friends & Family
Leading vs Lagging Indicators Framework
Leading = prediz futuro
Lagging = mostra passado (goals, resultados)
Track Everything Meticulously - "If you can't measure it, you can't manage it" (Peter Drucker)
MONEY QUADRANT:
Sales Team KPI Tracking:
Calls booked/completed
No-show rate
Close rates (total vs spoke to)
Sleep hours (Oura Ring/Whoop)
Daily meditation
Mood tracking
Holistic Sales Management:
Control entire life of salespeople
Track sleep, meditation, mood
Leading indicators predict close rate drops
Social Media Growth System:
Track daily: YouTube videos, Instagram feed, "Flex Pitch" stories, TikTok posts
80%+ consistency = predictable growth
Years of data = precise predictions
HEALTH & FITNESS QUADRANT:
Daily Tracking (Notion):
Calories consumed
Calories burned (Oura Ring)
Protein grams (1g/lb body weight target)
Water consumption
Weight (scale in product bag)
Workouts completed
Sleep hours (7-8 target)
Automatic Formulas:
Calorie deficit calculations
Monthly averages
Year-over-year comparisons
SEXUAL RELATIONSHIPS QUADRANT:
Five Love Languages System:
Identify partner's primary language
Track accordingly
Quality Time Tracking (girlfriend's love language):
Daily hours together
Target: 3.5 hours/dia
Actual example: 2.7h/dia (flagged as low)
Conflict Tracking:
Days without "tripping"
90% good month benchmark
Other metrics: (cropped out, not disclosed)
80% Rule: Sales performance drops = relationship problems
FRIENDS & FAMILY QUADRANT:
Son Time Tracking:
Bi-weekly Atlanta visits
All events attendance
Quantified time together
Friends ("The Homies") Tracking:
Regular time allocation
Scheduled hangouts
SKILL ACQUISITION SYSTEM:
Hour-Based Goals (not arbitrary):
Set target skill level
Calculate hours needed
Track daily progress
Reframe Progress:
"I'm not supposed to be good yet"
Match expectations to hours invested
Current Skills Tracked:
Guitar (1h/dia, 5-6 AM)
Spanish
Other undisclosed practices
TIME MANAGEMENT:
168 Hours/Week Exercise:
Calculate: sleep + work + workout + family/partner
Subtract from 168
Reveals available time
Calendar as Tetris:
Plan every hour
Recurring tasks (majority)
Color coding: Red/Pink = unmovable
Fit new items in white space
Sunday Planning:
Review week ahead
Adjust recurring tasks
Expect changes during week
Focus on Today:
Make today a victory
Don't attach emotions to distant goals
Leading indicators = daily wins
BOUNDARY SYSTEMS:
Phone on DND Always:
Only 3 people can interrupt
2 phones strategy (before DND features)
Text-first policy for everyone
Self-Filtering:
"Act like everything is dishwasher safe"
People who can't adapt filter themselves out
Maintained since "poor boy" days
No Grocery Shopping/Cleaning:
Delivery services
Hired cleaning team
Girlfriend manages (Spanish-speaking immigrants)
SALES TEAM MANAGEMENT:
Daily 8 AM Meetings:
Role play drilling
Don't practice on paid leads
Practice harder than games (Michael Jordan/Bulls philosophy)
Psychological Management:
Most salespeople close <50%
Missing most shots = psychological damage
Role plays = guaranteed wins
Build confidence before calls
Call Review:
Watch recordings
Identify patterns
Drill specific weaknesses
Morning Timing:
Ensure "on point" before calls
Pump up energy/confidence
HAWTHORNE EFFECT APPLICATION:
Performance Under Observation:
Factory productivity study
Being watched = better performance
Self-Observation:
Tracking = observing yourself
Hard to eat bad food when logging
Early problem detection
Adjustment before visible issues
Confidence Building:
Evidence of follow-through
Feel good before results
"I'm doing what I said"
ANTI-BULLSHIT LIST:
❌ Dopamine detox
❌ NoFap
❌ Making bed
❌ Affirmations (limited utility)
❌ Ayahuasca retreats
❌ Red light therapy
❌ Astrology/"crystal energy"
❌ "Bro version of astrology"
ESTRUTURA:
FOUR QUADRANTS FRAMEWORK:
Money (business/income)
Health & Fitness (physical)
Sexual Relationships (romantic partner)
Friends & Family (social/familial)
LEADING VS LAGGING INDICATORS:
Lagging: Goals, championships, money in bank, muscle, weight
Leading: Daily actions that create lagging results
Formula: Find leading indicators → Track meticulously → Adjust based on results
NOTION TRACKING SYSTEM:
Automated formulas
Daily entries
Monthly averages/sums
Year-over-year comparisons
Separate sheets per quadrant
Color-coded priorities
CALENDAR SYSTEM:
Red: Unmovable (meetings with self/others)
Pink: Girlfriend time (unmovable)
Other colors: Movable/flexible
Recurring: Majority of schedule
White space: Available for new items
Team access: Non-red items can be overridden
SKILL LEVELS MATRIX:
FIVE LOVE LANGUAGES:
Quality Time (girlfriend & sales guy's wife example)
Gifts (easiest for entrepreneurs - "can always buy more")
Words of Affirmation
Acts of Service
Physical Touch
QUOTES & PRINCIPLES:
Peter Drucker: "If you can't measure it, you can't manage it"
Gym Jesus King Keo: "If you're not tracking, you slacking"
Allan G (Northwestern/Columbia professor): "The less control people have over their lives, the more likely they are to try to regain control through mental gymnastics"
Dishwasher Safe Principle: "Act like everything is dishwasher safe, eventually everything will be"
Malcolm Gladwell: 10,000 hour rule (Outliers)
Chicago Bulls: Practice harder than games
PROBLEM CATEGORIES:
Ads not converting
Sales team numbers low
Coaching staff issues
Infrastructure for growth
Lead volume mismatches
"Whack-a-mole" constant shifting problems
HIRING PHILOSOPHY:
Brandon admits: "bad at hiring"
CMO handles operations
Had "historically bad hires"
Someone like CMO should have been hired earlier
Control salesperson's entire life
"Treat them like babies or worse"
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Contracted Revenue (exemplo mensal): $800K
Cash Collected Upfront: $600K
Personal Close Rate: 80% (anos fazendo vendas)
Team Close Rate: 59% (falaram) / 28% (total com no-shows)
No-Show Rate: 15%
Sales Since: 16 anos de idade
Milhões anuais: Confirmado, anos consecutivos
Average American Comparison: <$1,000 banco, 17 lbs overweight
Grocery/Cleaning: $0 (não faz há década, serviços terceirizados)
Time Value Optimization: Não especificado mas maximizado via delegação
DURAÇÃO: ~49 minutos FORMATO: Mastermind presentation / Sistema de vida quantificado com Q&A interativo
